title: BABIES FIRST! - 211info
text: CLACKAMAS COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 367 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (503) 742-5358 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 742-5300 Main phone (503) 655-8411 Disease Control and Prevention Reporting Main phone (503) 742-5358 Cassie Stewart [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or visit website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Varies by site LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm Services: Case/Care Management * Families with Infants/Toddlers Home Based Parenting Education Nutrition Education * Families with Infants/Toddlers Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - FOOD HANDLERS CARD ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - WATER QUALITY ASSURANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE PROGRAM PUBLIC HEALTH - PUBLIC FACILITY HEALTH INSPECTIONS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CLACKAMAS COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH
